summary: Patched /Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md end to end to address all six review findings while preserving the plan’s original intent.
markdown_response:
Patched only `/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md`.

Formal review after the patch: all six findings are resolved. The plan now has an explicit per-family mode matrix, daily defaults no longer imply a full non-L2 rebuild, execution-ready success is separated from skipped ML-ready via `training_context_ml_ready_dir`, ML training artifacts have a hard cutoff plus prediction-only artifact separation, legacy `--refresh-monthly` and `--refresh-raw-store` are preserved as transition aliases, date resolution cross-checks SSE with Tushare `trade_cal` and consumed endpoint freshness, and integration tests now use AST/module-boundary assertions instead of a brittle deprecated-name grep.

Validation: re-read the full plan, ran targeted `rg` checks for the required concepts and old-risk wording, and checked repo status. The repo has pre-existing unrelated changes under `/Users/roy/Documents/quant`; I did not edit them. No code tests were run because this task only changed the markdown plan.
resolved_findings: High: Daily default slow/flaky full non-L2 rebuild risk resolved by adding a concrete Mode Matrix with execute/probe/reuse_existing/skip_optional/block_if_stale actions and daily defaults that execute only daily/execution-required families.

High: Execution-ready and ML-ready coupling resolved by adding `training_context_ml_ready_dir`, forbidding skipped current ML-ready labels as execution inputs, and requiring execution manifests to distinguish daily execution inputs from optional historical training context.

High: Label completeness boundary resolved by requiring a hard training cutoff before writing `features.parquet`, `labels.parquet`, and `splits.parquet`, prediction-only rows in a separate artifact boundary, and ML gate assertions on `max(signal_date) <= ml_training_label_end`.
Medium: Public interface compatibility resolved by preserving `--refresh-monthly` and `--refresh-raw-store` as deprecated aliases with warnings and compatibility tests.
Medium: SSE-only date resolution resolved by requiring SSE calendar plus Tushare `trade_cal` cross-checking and consumed endpoint freshness verification before a date is treated as complete.
Low: Integration test false-positive risk resolved by replacing broad deprecated-name absence wording with AST/module-boundary assertions against production orchestrator internals.
